newsletters.subject: # Saturday DeepDive: The Battle for AI...
newsletters.summary: Superintelligence newsletter's Saturday DeepDive edition (paid tier). Single feature article: 'The Battle for AI Silicon: Who Controls the Chips That Power the Intelligence Revolution?' by Kim Isenberg. Covers NVIDIA's platform dominance (~$81.6B/quarter revenue, CUDA + NVLink moat), Google TPU v8t/8i split, AMD/Cerebras/Groq challengers, and China/Huawei parallel ecosystem. Substantial preview available but full analysis behind paywall. Topic is AI hardware infrastructure — tangentially relevant to wiki's inference/training infrastructure domain but primarily a market/strategy analysis piece rather than LLM/Agent technical knowledge. The remaining 14 links are social media profiles, subscription pages, and beehiiv tracking meta.

classified_articles.resolved_title: The Battle for AI Silicon: Who Controls the Chips That Power the Intelligence Revolution?
classified_articles.author: Kim Isenberg
classified_articles.reason: AI hardware market analysis. Covers NVIDIA's $81.6B revenue, CUDA/NVLink moats, Google TPU v8t/8i, AMD/Cerebras/Groq/China challenges. Tangentially relevant to wiki's 'inference/training infrastructure' domain but primarily a business/market piece. Paywalled — only NVIDIA section fully available.
key_data_points: NVIDIA Q1 FY2027: $81.6B revenue, $75.2B Data Center (+92% YoY)
Global AI compute doubling every ~7 months (Epoch AI)
DeepSeek V4 running natively on Chinese chips
NVIDIA roadmap: Blackwell Ultra (2026) → Vera Rubin (2H 2026) → Rubin Ultra (2027, up to 1TB HBM4e)
